title: LoL and the Dead Sea
wikitext:
[]: LoL and the Dead Sea             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Wed Apr 18, 2007 11:42 am Hi I'm new here so please don't kill me for this. I'm not sure if there was already a topic on this so if there is my apologizes. I was just rewatching Episode 12 and I noticed something. When they switch to the view of the carrier in the South Pole with Gendo and Fuyutsuki there is a covered pole that looks an awful lot like the Lance. Also soon after that Fuyutsuki mentions that this is truly the "Dead Sea." I had always thought that they found the Lance in the Dead Sea literally but if they mean this figuratively then they could actually mean that they found it here where Adam was originally. I would show the picture but I have no clue how to take a screen capture. Maybe Reichu could get it, she seems to be all powerful with this stuff from what I hear.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Apr 18, 2007 11:52 am my basic understanding says that they found the lance in the "real" dead sea, the Katsuragi research team took it to the pole, they used it when trying to contain adam, second impact happened, then they recover the lance. Fuyutski doesn't mean they are actually in the dead sea, but rather after second impact, the artic ocean is the true dead sea because ... everything is dead.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 18, 2007 4:06 pm slothen wrote: my basic understanding says that they found the lance in the "real" dead sea, the Katsuragi research team took it to the pole, they used it when trying to contain adam, second impact happened, then they recover the lance. Fuyutski doesn't mean they are actually in the dead sea, but rather after second impact, the artic ocean is the true dead sea because ... everything is dead.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:00 pm Let's see who the first person to say, "It's the 'Spear' not the 'Lance'" will be.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:10 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: Let's see who the first person to say, "It's the 'Spear' not the 'Lance'" will be.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:11 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: BrikHaus81 wrote: Let's see who the first person to say, "It's the 'Spear' not the 'Lance'" will be.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Apr 18, 2007 5:43 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: It was found with Adam, moved to the Dead Sea, moved back to the White Moon, and finally moved to Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Apr 18, 2007 6:16 pm slothen wrote: whats the point of taking it to the dead sea?

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Thu Apr 19, 2007 6:32 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: It was found with Adam, moved to the Dead Sea, moved back to the White Moon, and finally moved to Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Apr 19, 2007 8:39 am TemporalKnight wrote: Anonymous_Evafan wrote: It was found with Adam, moved to the Dead Sea, moved back to the White Moon, and finally moved to Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 19, 2007 4:41 pm Shin-seiki wrote: Adam was simultaneously blown to smithereens and reduced to an embryo as a result of 2I. (Don't ask me how that works. Fanwank something...).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 19, 2007 5:19 pm Most of that idea works for me. Though I still have trouble wrapping my brain around the logistics. I mean, did, like, most of Adam just instanteously blow off, and a little embryonic Adam enigmatically remain in the very center? The more I think about it, the less sense it makes. And don't forget about the Kaworu egg and how that needed to pull through the mess unscathed. BTW, OMF, if you are bychance intimating that bits of Adam flesh flew off and became Angels or something, I would be forced to give you the Psy-y-y .

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Apr 19, 2007 7:42 pm Reichu wrote: BTW, OMF, if you are bychance intimating that bits of Adam flesh flew off and became Angels or something, I would be forced to give you the Psy-y-y .

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 19, 2007 8:00 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Not entirely sure what you're giving me means

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Thu Apr 19, 2007 8:27 pm Reichu wrote: "Return Adam to an embryo before the other Angels awakened", and Seele knew that there were only fourteen of them (counting Israfel as one).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 19, 2007 9:11 pm TemporalKnight wrote: If there were only 14 Angels, then when Kaworu comes why does he go through his whole spiel about how only one species can survive. Now if Kaworu is the last Angel besides Adam then why bother?

[]:             Quote Post by TemporalKnight » Thu Apr 19, 2007 10:15 pm Reichu wrote: Adam was probably supposed to crank out more than these fourteen (or alt. fifteen), but, given the way events transpired, Adam was lucky to do as much as she did. So Adam conceivably laid fourteen eggs before being knocked out by the Spear, and finally gave souls to the unborn upon awakening (Doors of Guf, etc.).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Apr 19, 2007 11:10 pm TemporalKnight wrote: Apparently I have not surfed these sites long enough as I have not come across the "Kaworu can be like Adam just as Rei can be like Lilith" Theory.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 19, 2007 11:32 pm TemporalKnight wrote: Granted, Lilith was the intruder to begin with but she must have beaten Adam at some point back in the past.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Apr 20, 2007 8:20 pm i always thought that the Katsuragi team was up at the south pole, found the lance, but then found Adam, and she was messing everything up there, so they tried to use the Lance to subdue her, which still caused a large explosion (2I), and they lost the Lance. then they found it again 15 years later (ep 12) and returned it, then it got thrown to the moon by Unit 00, but later in EoE, Unit 01 recovered it. and so on. wait, how did Kanji get Adam's Embryo? well, how did they recover it from the "Dead Sea"?

I'm not sure if there was already a topic on this so if there is my apologizes. I was just rewatching Episode 12 and I noticed something. When they switch to the view of the carrier in the South Pole with Gendo and Fuyutsuki there is a covered pole that looks an awful lot like the Lance.
